slides.title: Sports and Health
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 6 English, Unit 2: Sports and Health. Welcome to Unit 2: Sports and Health! In this unit, you will explore the exciting world of sports, fitness, and healthy living. You will read about how athletes train, what makes a healthy lifestyle, and how sports bring communities together across East Africa and beyond. Along the way, you will learn powerful new vocabulary, master an important grammar structure called the First Conditional, and develop your writing and speaking skills through creative projects like writing a biography and delivering sports commentary. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Use and understand 28 new vocabulary words related to science, the environment, and health
Identify and use the First Conditional to talk about possible future events and their results
Use unless as an alternative to if not in conditional sentences
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to use and understand 28 new vocabulary words related to science, the environment, and health. you will also identify and use the first conditional to talk about possible future events and their results. you will also use unless as an alternative to if not in conditional sentences. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: geographic, originally, imagination, agency, prophet, deteriorate. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Sports and Health: source text 1 and Sports and Health: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: What Is the First Conditional?
bullets: The First Conditional is a way of talking about things that are likely or possible in the future. We use it when we believe there is a real chance that...
slides.narration: The first language focus is What Is the First Conditional?. The First Conditional is a way of talking about things that are likely or possible in the future. We use it when we believe there is a real chance that something will happen, and we want to describe what the result will be. For example: Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Sports Interview Role-Play. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
